title: Using weak hashing algorithms is security-sensitive
description: Using weak hashing algorithms is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotcweCryptographic hash algorithms such asMD2,MD4,MD5,MD6,HAVAL-128,DSA(which usesSHA-1),RIPEMD,RIPEMD-128,RIPEMD-160andSHA-1are no longer considered secure, because it is possible to havecollisions(little computational effort is enough to find two or more different inputs that produce the same hash).Message authentication code (MAC) algorithms such asHMAC-MD5orHMAC-SHA1use weak hash functions as building blocks. Although they are not all proven to be weak, they are considered legacy algorithms and should be avoided.Ask Yourself WhetherThe hashed value is used in a security context like:User-password storage.Security token generation (used to confirm e-mail when registering o
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Using weak hashing algorithms is security-sensitive. Cryptographic hash algorithms such as MD2, MD4, MD5, MD6, HAVAL-128, SHA-1, and RIPEMD are no longer considered secure due to collision vulnerabilities. Use SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, or SHA-3 instead.
